form.name: Name
form.localPath: Local clone path (worktrees branch from here)

home.tagline: Local batch PR review. Terminal agent at the core, web for gatekeeping and state management.
home.ghStatusNotReady: Not ready — run gh auth login first
home.emptyHint: Create your first project with + on the left.

project.noTasks: No review tasks yet. Go to “All PRs” and check some PRs to start.
project.authorUpdatedTitle: The author pushed new commits after your last comment — you can recheck
project.severityTitle: High / medium / low severity issue counts

clean.confirmMsg: Delete {n} “{label}” review task(s)? (local tasks only, GitHub untouched)

confirm.deleteTaskMsg: Delete the review task for #{pr}? (local task only, GitHub comment untouched)

project.refreshAllTitle: Refresh GitHub state of all tasks (approved / merged / author updated)
project.noneReviewable: All selected PRs already have a review task
project.noneFixable: All selected PRs already have an active fix task
project.noFixes: No fix tasks yet. Pick PRs in "All PRs" and click "Fix selected".

prDrawer.title: PR details
prDrawer.diffTruncated: Diff too large, truncated — see the full content on GitHub.

config.modelSection: Review model (actually available on your local claude)
config.noEffortSupport: This model does not support effort settings
config.localPathRequired: Set the local clone path to use AI generation.
config.noSkills: No skills yet. Click “AI generate” to have the AI read your project and produce one, or “+ Blank” to write it by hand. With no skill enabled, the built-in default methodology is used.
config.candidateGenerated: New candidate generated — preview/compare, then click “Enable”.
config.genInterrupted: Generation request interrupted — if a new candidate appears above it was generated, otherwise retry.

confirm.deleteProjectMsg: Delete project “{name}”? Review tasks and skills will be deleted too (no impact on GitHub).

warnModal.title: Skill health check
warnModal.body: “{name}” contains terms that look like an “operations flow”. A review skill should only describe “what to audit, how to judge”, not git operations / code changes / worktree skipping (handled by the engine). Matched:
warnModal.note:
Most are “descriptive mentions” (false positives — e.g. writing “no git push” in the methodology also gets flagged), with no impact.
Even when enabled, the engine hard-blocks git writes / file changes at the tool level; a skill cannot run them — so this is just a warning, not an error.

lintModal.title: This skill may contain operations-flow content
lintModal.body: “{name}” matched the following suspected “operations flow” content (review only audits, never modifies; operations are handled by the engine):
lintModal.note: Note: these may be descriptive mentions (false positives). Even when enabled, the engine hard-blocks git writes / file changes at the tool level, which won't actually run.

genModal.intro: The AI uses the project's configured model/effort, reads the full local repo + thinks deeply, then produces output. {mode}The result is saved as a new candidate — it does not overwrite or auto-enable.
genModal.instructionLabel: Instruction for the AI (optional, leave empty for the default approach)
genModal.instructionPlaceholder: E.g.: emphasize permissions and concurrency; follow the staki-review format; watch tRPC input validation; use a stricter tone…

newModal.title: New review skill
newModal.contentLabel: Content (methodology, can stay empty for later / paste an existing one)

review.noTask: This PR has no review task yet
review.authorUpdatedTitle: The author pushed new commits after your last comment
review.recheckConfirm: Check the new commits after the author's comments?
review.rerunTitle: Keeps your checks/notes; targeted recheck using each note + the instruction below, the AI replies point by point
review.recheckTitle: Reads the author's new commits since your last comment and judges whether each item was addressed
review.retryTitle: Run a full review from scratch — clears current findings and notes
review.freshConfirm: Re-run from scratch? Current findings and notes will be erased.
review.instructionPlaceholder: What the AI should focus on / where you disagree / have it recheck something. “Recheck per my feedback” reviews this + each note.
review.notePlaceholder: Reply / feedback (to the AI: invalid? add context? have it recheck? With “Recheck per my feedback”, the AI responds. When checked, translated to English in the posted comment)
review.globalNotesPlaceholder: Placed at the top of the comment, translated to English when posting
review.regenTitle: Ignore the cache, regenerate from the current findings/notes

fix.modalHint: Validates comments with the built-in instruction by default. Add targeted guidance below (optional).
fix.instructionPlaceholder: e.g. only security-related comments; I disagree with this one, double-check…
fix.discardConfirm: Discard this fix task? (clears the local worktree, GitHub untouched)
fix.notePlaceholder: Fix instruction (for the AI: how to fix this / extra context). Used when you run the fix.
fix.pushOthersHint: Can only push your own PR
fix.pushConfirm: Will push the changes across {files} file(s) to {branch}.
fix.replyConfirm: Will reply to the author: {fixed} fixed, {wontfix} explained as won't-fix.
fix.replyFailed: Some replies couldn't be posted (threads may be resolved/deleted)
fix.chatPlaceholder: Keep chatting / ask the AI to change more (e.g. rewrite F3; missing null check here…)
fix.runFixResetWarn: Re-running resets the worktree to the PR base — changes made in chat will be lost. Continue?
fix.chatHint: Run a fix first, then you can keep chatting here to refine it.
fix.runFixAfterPushHint: Already pushed; use the chat below to keep changing
fix.mergeBaseTitle: Merge the PR's target branch to resolve conflicts (git run by the engine)
fix.mergeConflicts: {count} conflicting file(s) with {base} — ask the AI in chat to resolve
fix.mergeChatDraft: Resolve the merge conflicts in these files: {files}.
fix.conflictBanner: Merge conflicts with the base branch need resolving — tell the AI how in chat
